subject: Re: Subtomograms constantly coming out black
body:
Hi Nadav,

when you say "small (50 pixel) boxes", do you mean same pixel size but 
smaller box, or same physical box size but larger pixels? Have you tried 
the nightly build <http://deployment.warpem.com/nightly/Warp_20200901.zip>? 
1.0.9 introduced a new mechanism to avoid CTF aliasing in sub-tomo 
reconstruction which will pad the box to an aliasing-free size (which 
depends on defocus and pixel size), reconstruct, and crop to the selected 
box size. The aliasing-free size can get really large. The updated version 
puts an upper limit on that at the expense of possible aliasing if you get 
closer to the Nyquist limit.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Monday, September 14, 2020 at 8:06:29 AM UTC+2 nada...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi,
> My reconstructed subtomograms are constantly coming out black, i.e with 
> voxel values of zero. Would appreciate any idea how to solve this.
>
> I am running Warp on a workstation with Windows 10 and 4 Nvidia 1080 Ti 
> GPU cards, so I don't see a memory issue. Reconstructing small (50 pixel) 
> boxes does not help. The tomograms are of purified ribosomes, collected 
> using SerialEM with phase plate. Movies were motion corrected and summed in 
> Warp, followed by alignments of the tilts using IMOD, as described in the 
> tutorial. The tomograms reconstruct nicely in Warp and I used template 
> matching to locate the particles, which seem to give nice peaks, and the 
> resulting star file looks OK. I tried all the options in the subtomogram 
> reconstruction menu, but subtomos and 3D CTFs are always black. Image 
> series option results are fine, containing proper density.
>
> There was a suggestion in the forum to change the regional settings of 
> Windows 10, but it did not help here.
>
> I will be happy to share any file related to this project.
>
> Thank you
> Nadav
>
